summary: Option A was pre-locked and deployed: ollama-bridge now runs on GKE and the Service resolves, but no Ollama backend pod or Service was deployed. Routing to the bridge is proven; actual Ollama inference returns the expected backend-missing error and the travel runtime falls back to OpenAI.

1_decide_and_inspect.option_chosen: A: bridge only, no Ollama backend
1_decide_and_inspect.rationale: User explicitly pre-selected option A. This is a minimal routing placeholder that proves the in-cluster bridge URL resolves before a backend decision is made.

2_deploy_to_gke.method: helm upgrade noetl automation/helm/noetl --reuse-values with ollamaBridge.enabled=true and bridge image pinned to ghcr.io/noetl/noetl:v2.37.8

bridge_routing.bridge_logs: POST /jsonrpc HTTP/1.1 200 OK entries observed from worker/source pods

travel_ollama_help.provider_fallback_reason: {"code":-32030,"message":"ollama /api/chat failed: Cannot connect to host ollama.noetl.svc.cluster.local:11434 ssl:default [Name or service not known]","data":{"upstream_url":"http://ollama.noetl.svc.cluster.local:11434/api/chat"}}

audit_rows.travel_agent_events: classify_intent row recorded for execution 624832446000792195 with ai_provider=openai and intent=help after fallback

4_close_out.ops_gitlink_bump: not_needed; deployment used existing merged Helm templates and values

notes: This is not native Ollama inference GREEN. With no backend deployed, --provider ollama must fall back to OpenAI after the bridge reports the missing upstream Ollama service.
The useful proof from this round is that the GKE bridge Service exists, resolves from worker pods, accepts JSON-RPC requests, and is reached by the travel runtime's Ollama provider path.
deferred_followups: Provision Ollama backend (CPU pod or external endpoint) — option B or C from the original Round B design when the time is right.
